violations.text: Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the milk cooler. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the milk cooler. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings and gnawed food in the back storage room. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Inspector notified principal of pest activity and provided the pest management in schools guidance document. Facilites began cleaning storage room during the time of inspection. Adulterated foods and single service articles were voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the milk cooler. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2025

The floor and storage shelves in the back storage area has an accumulation of droppings, soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/02/2025

The double door to the outside, located in the hallway, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the milk cooler door gaskets and the window ledges have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024
The floors in the establishment have areas of poor repair where old equipment used to sit. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Corrective Action - Establishment is to sanitize using the three bay sink (quat measured at 200ppm) until machine is in working order. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the Quat sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the gaskets on the three door fridge have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces of the area behind the three bay have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces of the floor and wall junction behind the dishwasher have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
The floors in the establishment have areas of poor repair were old equipment used to sit. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment needs to have a person in charge registered with RIDOH-CFP. PIC is licensed at FSV27885. Each School is a separate Food Business License and therefore shall have a registered Food Safety Manager on duty at all times when food is being prepared. Application was provided at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/01/2024

upon arrival inspector observed cheese sitting on counter. Temperature of cheese was taken. Cheese was observed to be 65 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Voluntary disposal of cheese was made at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2023

Single service items (foil pans) are not being stored properly. Foil Pans out of packaging were observed on floor in dry storage. Single service/use articles shall be stored in their original protective package and 6 inches off the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the floor along coving near 3 door freezer (dry storage), near exterior doorway in dry storage, along coving behind stove and near 3 bay sink was observed to have an accumulation of an unidentifiable debris. Areas needs to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2023
